_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of encryption algorithms, stenography, and other forms of data concealment.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific privacy regulations, with emphasis on the legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement precedent.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.516), RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.499).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.245). Shared vocabulary: [data, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [algorithms, concealment, encryption, forms, stenography] vs WIDAI-only terms [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, emphasis] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.886, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.036 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (87.5%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of encryption algorithms, stenography, and other forms of data conceal" and "Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.529 > STS 0.245 (gap +0.284). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.1%. Shared tokens: [data, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [algorithms, concealment, encryption, forms, stenography]. WIDAI-only: [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, emphasis].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 87.5% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.284 with low STS 0.245. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
